name: delegate_to_agent
description: Delegate a self-contained task to another local AI agent. Returns IMMEDIATELY with a task_id; the sub-agent runs in the background. Collect the result with get_delegation_status(task_id) — pass wait_ms to block until it finishes, or wait_ms=0 to poll. You can fan out several delegate_to_agent calls first and then collect each. Use cancel_delegation(task_id) to stop one. The sub-agent runs in a separate session without access to the current conversation, so include all required context in `task`.

agent_type.description: Agent type to launch as the sub-agent for this delegated task.

task.description: Complete task prompt for the sub-agent, including the goal, relevant context, constraints, and expected output. The sub-agent cannot see this conversation.

working_dir.description: Absolute directory where the sub-agent should run. Defaults to the parent session's current working directory.

name: get_delegation_status
description: Check the status of a delegated task by its task_id (returned by delegate_to_agent), and optionally block until it finishes. With wait_ms > 0 the call blocks up to that many milliseconds and returns the result as soon as the sub-agent finishes (capped at 60s — re-call to keep waiting); with wait_ms = 0 (default) it returns the current status immediately. A finished task returns its full result text; for a long result, open the child session for the complete output.

wait_ms.description: Max milliseconds to block waiting for the task to finish before returning the current status. 0 (default) returns immediately. Capped at 60000.

name: cancel_delegation
description: Cancel a running delegated task by its task_id (returned by delegate_to_agent). Stops the sub-agent. A task that already finished returns its terminal result instead.
